heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 18 July 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 2nd since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 19 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 03 July 2026.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwave at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 heatwaves, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and this has only occurred 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (206°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 8.0°C) and outside temperature (down 6.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 19%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 2525 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1759 m) and outside humidity (down 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2520m lower than yesterday, 1758.7m lower than the past week, 1044.2m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1974m to a maximum of 8460m, averaging 2660m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4420m and 3710m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: A high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; pleasant, mild temperatures. Also, dry and less humid conditions and very little to no wind will be present. Showers, a high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions, and it’s dark. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; there will not be a great deal of drying overnight.
